explicit_text: I was born in Edgefield, South Carolina, in 1902, right on Columbia Street, about three quarters of a mile from the courthouse. We lived in town initially, but when I was about four, my father moved us out to the country so the boys could learn farm life. Edgefield was the backdrop of my childhood, with farms and neighbors nearby, shaping much of my early experiences.

That town was the backdrop of my childhood, with farms and neighbors nearby, shaping much of my early experiences.

explicit_text: In 1916, when I was fourteen, we bought our first car, a Dodge automobile. Before that, we mostly got around by buggy, especially when visiting the farms in the country. That Dodge was quite a milestone for us, marking the transition from horse-drawn buggies to motor vehicles in our family.

explicit_text: During World War I, around 1917, one of my neighbors went off to war, and I bought his crop to help him out. explicit_text: My father grew up near Hawkins Creek, which lies between Steven's Creek and the Savannah River. He used to ride six to eight miles to school every day on horseback from there. That rural upbringing influenced his character and work ethic throughout his life.
